mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously during crisis periods, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as liquidity conditions improve.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% on 2009-03-01 indicates emergency easing
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 8.0% and continues climbing month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off behavior. This threshold-crossing condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE acceleration and negative returns falls below 0.15 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600k units, indicating synchronized demand destruction across consumer and residential sectors. This joint deterioration predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344.38B on 2009-03-01, HOUST at 583k on 2009-02-01
failure_condition: Interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window, or SPX returns are not significantly lower when both conditions trigger versus single-series triggers alone (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 98.0 index level and remains below its 3-month moving average, signaling supply-side contraction that reduces corporate profit margins. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions flow downward.
regime_hint: supply-side recession with capacity utilization collapse
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (mean return > -1% or t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month by more than 500k persons while unemployment rate simultaneously rises, creating a synchronized labor market shock. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession fears intensify.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < -500k AND UNRATE month-over-month change > +0.3pp
regime_hint: acute labor market shock during financial crisis
failure_condition: Joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 24-month window, or SPX returns are not significantly more negative when both conditions trigger versus either condition alone (t-test p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month while federal funds rate remains near zero, indicating deflation risk that increases real debt burdens and equity risk premiums. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as deflation expectations anchor.
regime_hint: deflationary crisis with zero lower bound monetary policy
failure_condition: Deflation-signal produces Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month window, or SPX returns are not significantly lower during deflation episodes versus inflation episodes (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth turns negative quarter-over-quarter, signaling economic contraction that reduces forward corporate earnings. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession is confirmed and earnings multiples compress.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11522.1B on 2008-10-01, revised to 16485.35B indicating data revision risk
regime_hint: confirmed recession with negative GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters with negative GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 20-quarter rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) falls below 90.0 index level while simultaneously payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines, indicating synchronized supply and labor destruction. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as stagflation or severe recession risks materialize.
regime_hint: severe supply-side recession with labor market collapse
failure_condition: Joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 24-month window, or SPX returns are not significantly more negative when both conditions trigger versus single-series triggers (t-test p > 0.10).
